statement.p: An object was launched from the top of a hill with an upward vertical velocity of 80 feet per second. The height of the object can be modeled by the function h(t)={-16t^{2}+80t+250}\text{,} where t represents the number of seconds after the launch. Assume the object landed on the ground at sea level. Find the answer using technology. Round your answers to two decimal places. If there is more than one answer, use a comma to separate them.
The object was 266 feet high at the following number of seconds after it was launched: .

solution.p: Use technology to graph the functions h(t)={-16t^{2}+80t+250} and y=266\text{,} and find their intersection points, (0.21,266) and (4.79,266)\text{.}
According to the two points of intersection, the object was 266 feet high at the following times after it was launched: 0.21 seconds and 4.79 seconds.
